description: Data-driven deprecated-terminology map for check-deprecated-terms.py. Each rule has a regex pattern, a replacement, and optional severity. Edit this file -- not the script -- to add/remove rules.
_warnings: Running `check-deprecated-terms.py --apply` applies rules in order. Cascading edits can produce awkward output: e.g. 'Azure Active Directory (Azure AD)' becomes 'Microsoft Entra ID (Microsoft Entra ID)' after both AAD rules fire. Review apply output before committing.
Rules at severity=low require human judgement; they are listed for awareness but should usually be reviewed in context before `--apply`.

rules.note: Renamed July 2023. Pattern also consumes a trailing '(Azure AD)' parenthetical when present to avoid double-replacement. First mention of the rename should usually be written by hand as 'Microsoft Entra ID (previously Azure Active Directory)'; automation replaces plain occurrences.
Short form of the old name. The 'is_already_fixed' guard in the checker skips cases immediately following 'Microsoft Entra ID (previously'.
Synapse capabilities are now part of Fabric. Review context before auto-replacing in migration / historical content.
Branding shift as of 2025. Review before auto-applying on pages that explain product history.
Licensing consolidation under Fabric. Verify commercial context before applying.
